metadata.summary: Comprehensive database selection using CAP theorem, PACELC, paradigm analysis, and polyglot persistence patterns across 100+ databases.

content.title: Database Selection Guide
content.summary: Comprehensive framework for choosing the right database among 100+ options. Relational databases for ACID transactions. NewSQL for distributed SQL. Document databases for flexible schema. Key-value stores for caching. Time-series databases for metrics. Graph databases for relationships. Vector databases for embeddings. Search engines for full-text search.

why.p: Database selection is the single most impactful architecture decision. The wrong choice locks you into technology for years. Database migrations cost 3-6 months and risk data corruption. CAP theorem constrains all distributed databases: Consistency+Partition Tolerance, Availability+Partition Tolerance, or Consistency+Availability.

list.item: Designing any new persistent data layer (greenfield or migration).
Choosing between database paradigms (relational, document, wide-column, graph, time-series).
Normalizing an existing schema with update anomalies or data duplication.
Modeling polyglot persistence: assigning each data domain to its optimal store.

rule.statement: Relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL) for ACID transactions. NewSQL (CockroachDB, TiDB, Spanner) for distributed SQL. Document (MongoDB, DynamoDB) for flexible schema. Key-Value (Redis, Memcached) for caching. Time-Series (TimescaleDB, InfluxDB) for metrics. Graph (Neo4j, Neptune) for relationships. Vector (Qdrant, Weaviate, Pinecone) for embeddings. Search (Elasticsearch, Meilisearch) for full-text search.

CP (Consistency + Partition Tolerance) — use when data accuracy matters (finance, banking). AP (Availability + Partition Tolerance) — use when always online matters (social media, streaming). CA (Consistency + Availability) — use when single region, network partitions unlikely.
